name: Touch Controls
description: A comprehensive mobile touch input system providing customizable on-screen controls with joysticks, buttons, and steering wheels. Automatically adapts to device type (mobile/tablet/desktop) and orientation (portrait/landscape) with per-device configuration. Supports dynamic scaling, EventBus integration, and runtime visibility control. Essential for mobile gameplay with full virtual input dispatching.

joystickEnabled.name: Enabled
joystickEnabled.description: Enable or disable the virtual joystick control for movement input.

joystickRunThreshold.name: Run Threshold
joystickRunThreshold.description: Threshold for running input (0-1). Above this value, character will run instead of walk.

joystickPosition.name: Position
joystickPosition.description: Position of the joystick on the screen, normalized to [0, 0] to [1, 1]

joystickHandleScale.name: Thumb Scale
joystickHandleScale.description: Scale factor for the joystick handle (thumb), 1 = default size

joystickBackgroundImage.name: Joystick Background
joystickBackgroundImage.description: Background image for the joystick base

steeringWheelEnabled.name: Enabled
steeringWheelEnabled.description: Enable or disable the virtual steering wheel control for rotational input.

steeringWheelPosition.name: Position
steeringWheelPosition.description: Position of the steering wheel on the screen, normalized to [0, 0] to [1, 1]

steeringWheelReturnSpeed.name: Return Speed
steeringWheelReturnSpeed.description: Speed at which wheel returns to center (1-20)

buttonEnabled.name: Enabled
buttonEnabled.description: Enable or disable this individual button.

buttonType.name: Type
buttonType.description: The action this button performs: Jump, Interact, a custom event, or a custom virtual input.

buttonPosition.name: Position
buttonPosition.description: Position of the button on the screen, normalized to [0, 0] to [1, 1]

onButtonPress.name: On Press Event
onButtonPress.description: Custom event to trigger when the button is pressed

customInputName.name: Custom Input
customInputName.description: Virtual InputManager action name to set while this button is pressed

onButtonRelease.name: On Release Event
onButtonRelease.description: Custom event to trigger when the button is released

editorLayoutPreview.name: Device Preview
editorLayoutPreview.description: Select which device layout to preview and edit in the editor

editorLayoutOrientation.name: Orientation Preview
editorLayoutOrientation.description: Preview orientation for the selected device layout in the editor

mobileEnabled.name: Enabled
mobileEnabled.description: Enable touch controls on mobile devices

mobileVerticalLayout.name: Vertical Layout
mobileVerticalLayout.description: Control layout used when the mobile device is in portrait orientation.

mobileHorizontalLayout.name: Horizontal Layout
mobileHorizontalLayout.description: Control layout used when the mobile device is in landscape orientation.

tabletEnabled.name: Enabled
tabletEnabled.description: Enable touch controls on tablet devices

tabletVerticalLayout.name: Vertical Layout
tabletVerticalLayout.description: Control layout used when the tablet is in portrait orientation.

tabletHorizontalLayout.name: Horizontal Layout
tabletHorizontalLayout.description: Control layout used when the tablet is in landscape orientation.

desktopEnabled.name: Enabled
desktopEnabled.description: Enable touch controls on desktop devices

desktopVerticalLayout.name: Vertical Layout
desktopVerticalLayout.description: Control layout used when a desktop browser window is in portrait orientation.

desktopHorizontalLayout.name: Horizontal Layout
desktopHorizontalLayout.description: Control layout used when a desktop browser window is in landscape orientation.

documentation:
## Touch Controls

A comprehensive mobile touch input system providing customizable on-screen controls with joysticks, buttons, and steering wheels. Automatically adapts to device type (mobile/tablet/desktop) and orientation (portrait/landscape) with per-device configuration. Supports dynamic scaling, EventBus integration, and runtime visibility control. Essential for mobile gameplay with full virtual input dispatching.

### Key Features
- Device-aware initialization: mobile, tablet, desktop detection
- Orientation-specific layouts: separate portrait and landscape configs
- Virtual joystick with configurable deadzone and run threshold
- Steering wheel control with rotation limits and return speed
- Multiple customizable buttons with icons and positions
- Responsive scaling system: width/height/min/max dimension modes
- Runtime visibility toggling via EventBus
- Dynamic show/hide for individual controls
- VirtualInputDispatcher integration for seamless input mapping
- Singleton behavior (one per scene)
- Debounced resize handling for smooth transitions
- Z-index management for proper layering
- Editor preview support with live attribute updates

### How It Works
1. Attach to scene (singleton, auto-manages touch controls)
2. Configure device enablement: mobileEnabled, tabletEnabled, desktopEnabled
3. For each enabled device, set portrait and landscape layouts
4. Scaling configuration:
   - scaleMode: none/width/height/min/max based on screen dimensions
   - targetScreenWidth/Height for reference resolution
5. Joystick setup:
   - Position (x, y in 0-1 screen space)
   - Size, deadzone, run threshold
   - Optional image customization
6. Steering wheel setup:
   - Position, size, sensitivity
   - maxRotation angle, return speed
   - Custom wheel image
7. Buttons array:
   - Each button: type (jump/interact/custom), position, size, icon
   - Configurable key bindings and custom events
8. On game start:
   - Detects device type via userAgent and touch support
   - Checks orientation (portrait vs landscape)
   - Loads appropriate layout configuration
   - Creates DOM elements for each control
   - Registers pointer event listeners via PointerEventManager
9. Runtime updates:
   - Window resize triggers debounced orientation check
   - If orientation changes, recreates controls with new layout
   - Scaler recalculates positions and sizes
10. Input flow:
    - Touch events captured by control elements
    - Joystick: calculates normalized direction vector (-1 to 1)
    - Buttons: dispatch virtual key press/release events
    - Steering wheel: rotation angle mapped to turn input
    - VirtualInputDispatcher translates to game input system
11. EventBus commands:
    - touchControls:enable/disable - toggle interactivity
    - touchControls:toggleVisibility - show/hide all controls
    - touchControls:showButton/hideButton - individual button control
    - touchControls:showJoystick/hideJoystick
    - touchControls:showSteeringWheel/hideSteeringWheel
12. Cleanup on stop: removes listeners, DOM elements

### Common Use Cases
- Mobile game virtual controls
- Tablet touch interface for desktop games
- Cross-platform input system
- Racing games with steering wheel controls
- Top-down or third-person mobile games
- Platformers with jump/interact buttons
- Custom gesture-based controls
- Responsive mobile UI for various screen sizes
- Games with multiple control schemes per device
- Hybrid touch/keyboard games

### When Not To Use
- For desktop-only games (no touch support needed)
- When using native device controls (gyroscope, tilt)
- For gesture-based games (swipe, pinch, etc.)
- When implementing custom touch UI from scratch
- For simple click/tap interactions (use pointer events directly)
- When controls should be in 3D world space
- For VR or AR applications
- When using external controller input only
- For games with no player movement/actions
- When building platform-specific native apps

### Technical Details
- Device detection: navigator.userAgent + maxTouchPoints
- Tablet detection: iPad, Android tablets, iPadOS 13+ Safari
- Mobile: iPhone, iPod, Android Mobile, Windows Phone patterns
- Orientation: window.innerHeight > innerWidth = portrait
- Resize debouncing: 100ms timeout to prevent rapid recreation
- Z-index hierarchy: HUD_Z_INDEX.TouchJoystick (-999), TouchButtons (-998)
- Scaling calculation: viewport dimensions vs target screen size
- Joystick: circular touch area, normalized vector output
- Deadzone: minimum joystick displacement before input registers
- Run threshold: joystick distance triggering run vs walk
- Steering wheel: rotation clamped to maxRotation (degrees)
- Return speed: rate at which wheel returns to center (per second)
- Button press: dispatches keydown event via VirtualInputDispatcher
- Button release: dispatches keyup event
- Custom events: EventBus.send() for game-specific actions
- DOM injection: elements added to document.body
- Pointer capture: uses PointerEventManager for unified touch/mouse
- Throttle: CRITICAL priority, always updates, no culling
- isSingleton: true enforces one instance per scene
- isThrottlingLocked: true prevents throttle config changes
- Editor support: TouchControlsEditor for live preview
- Cleanup: removes event listeners, DOM elements, subscriptions
- Layout switching: full teardown and recreation on orientation change
